title: Understanding the Forces
text: To find the tension in the ropes, consider the forces acting on the girl. The forces include the gravitational force, the horizontal force, and the tension in the ropes.

title: Components of Tension
text: The tension in the ropes can be broken down into two components: one vertical and one horizontal. The vertical component balances the gravitational force, and the horizontal component balances the horizontal force.

title: Using Trigonometry
text: Use trigonometric functions to relate the tension components to the angle of the ropes. The vertical component is $$T cos\left(30\right)$$ and the horizontal component is $$T sin\left(30\right)$$.

title: Calculate Vertical Component
text: Calculate the vertical component of the tension using the equation: $$T cos\left(30\right)=m g$$, where $$m$$ is the mass of the girl and g is the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Calculate Tension
text: Solve for T using the equation from the previous step.
